title: Shin evangelion for the 2022 oscars
wikitext:
[]: Shin evangelion for the 2022 oscars?             Quote Post by Thesufferingpumpkin » Mon Mar 15, 2021 8:24 am It may seem far fetched but does anyone think they will go for best animated feature film? The oscars seems to be more diverse than ever now

[]: Re: Shin evangelion for the 2022 oscars?             Quote Post by The_Max » Mon Mar 15, 2021 8:43 am Oscar to the most delayed film!

[]: Re: Shin evangelion for the 2022 oscars?             Quote Post by Jäeger » Mon Mar 15, 2021 9:45 am Thesufferingpumpkin wrote: It may seem far fetched but does anyone think they will go for best animated feature film? The oscars seems to be more diverse than ever now

[]: Re: Shin evangelion for the 2022 oscars?             Quote Post by Thesufferingpumpkin » Mon Mar 15, 2021 9:54 am Jäeger wrote: Thesufferingpumpkin#915674 wrote: It may seem far fetched but does anyone think they will go for best animated feature film? The oscars seems to be more diverse than ever now

[]: Re: Shin evangelion for the 2022 oscars?             Quote Post by JuanSorel » Mon Mar 15, 2021 10:26 am Thesufferingpumpkin wrote: Jäeger#915688 wrote: No

[]: Re: Shin evangelion for the 2022 oscars?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Mar 15, 2021 10:32 am The Oscars have always been dismissive of foreign animation; unless it's Ghibli being promoted by Disney, even short-listing is unusual. The negativity is just being realistic, regardless of the merits or otherwise of the film.

[]: Re: Shin evangelion for the 2022 oscars?             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Mon Mar 15, 2021 11:50 am Your Name got snubbed by the Academy; Mirai got nominated at least but it still lost to the usual schlock. That should tell you something about Shin Eva's chances.

[]: Re: Shin evangelion for the 2022 oscars?             Quote Post by Sharpe » Mon Mar 15, 2021 10:04 pm Don't see 3.0+1.0 even making the shortlist sadly. I think even if it does have some truly breathtaking traditional animation and CG, and I believe it will, it stands little to no chance. Definitely doesn't help that it is the fourth installment in a franchise that also semi-requires you to have seen a tv show and another movie. 99% of the Academy voters aren't exactly Eva fans I'd wager.

[]: Re: Shin evangelion for the 2022 oscars?             Quote Post by Saint Eva » Tue Mar 16, 2021 4:02 pm Despite somehow awarding Spirited Away back in 2002, Academy Award voters still do not care about anime. Tale of Princess Kaguya was nominated in 2015 Oscars but lost to Big Hero 6. Those voters were later surveyed by a reporter and they admitted that they don't watch all nominated movies. One of the voters was pissed that Lego movie got snubbed in favor of Princess Kaguya "freakin obscure Chinese movie that nobody ever saw". That's all you need to know about Evangelion chances for next year.

[]: Re: Shin evangelion for the 2022 oscars?             Quote Post by Jayfive » Tue Mar 16, 2021 5:20 pm EXTREMELY unlikely to happen: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Academy_A ... ed_Feature

[]: Re: Shin evangelion for the 2022 oscars?             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Mar 17, 2021 1:40 am I doubt it, considering none of the previous films did and this film isn't somehow more special than they were-- it's a feat of animation, sure, but does it really have "academy" written on it? It's the fourth film of a tetrology that has to compete in what is effectively a best film category, so it can't win on its visuals alone. Or its sound design, or its voice acting, or anything like that. Something tells me it will lose out to a more self-contained animated or foreign film (if they went that route). If we want to look at precedent, only a handful of foreign films have even gotten nominated in the best animated feature category and they've all been standalone films. The only sequels that have even been nominated have been from large American studios and the only winners have been from Pixar. (Toy Story 3 and Toy Story 4.) Eva likely doesn't have Oscar dreams, but even people who work in the industry will tell you that the whole point of the Oscars is for the movie industry to pat itself on the back for what a wonderful job it did. Foreign live-action films are an afterthought at best, and foreign animation is unfortunately treated similarly. Editing to add that Spirited Away did win in 2003, but it was also up against an extraordinarily weak field: Ice Age, Treasure Planet, Lilo and Stitch (not as weak), and Spirit: Stallion of the Cimarron. (And are we really comparing NTE to Spirited Away?)

[]: Re: Shin evangelion for the 2022 oscars?             Quote Post by Jäeger » Wed Mar 17, 2021 3:50 am Thesufferingpumpkin wrote: Jäeger#915688 wrote: No

[]: Re: Shin evangelion for the 2022 oscars?             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Mar 17, 2021 4:21 am The "Best Animated Feature" category was made so that animated films couldn't threaten live-action films in the "Best Picture" category. It usually tends to be used by the Academy members as "Best Movie I Saw with My Grandchildren."

[]: Re: Shin evangelion for the 2022 oscars?             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Sun Sep 19, 2021 8:36 pm This film is too good for the Oscars ...is what I tell myself to try and not be angry at the fact that it won't ever be there.

[]: Re: Shin evangelion for the 2022 oscars?             Quote Post by RussianRiz » Sun Sep 19, 2021 8:40 pm I remember that Madoka's third film figured in the shortlist in 2014, which was a super curious fact. Considering how much marketing this movie is getting from Amazon, I wouldn't really be surprised if it was considered. Being a candidate for the final 5 selected is difficult. Winning just wouldn't happen.

[]: Re: Shin evangelion for the 2022 oscars?             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Sun Sep 19, 2021 8:57 pm There's too much egregious fanservice for it to be considered.

[]: Re: Shin evangelion for the 2022 oscars?             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Sun Sep 19, 2021 9:00 pm Axx°N N. wrote: There's too much egregious fanservice for it to be considered.

[]: Re: Shin evangelion for the 2022 oscars?             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Sun Sep 19, 2021 10:28 pm kuribo-04 wrote: I'm sure that's not the issue. I doubt sexual content is something Oscar members look down on.

[]: Re: Shin evangelion for the 2022 oscars?             Quote Post by HARVEST » Sun Sep 19, 2021 10:45 pm TheFriskyIan wrote: Your Name got snubbed by the Academy; Mirai got nominated at least but it still lost to the usual schlock. That should tell you something about Shin Eva's chances.

[]: Re: Shin evangelion for the 2022 oscars?             Quote Post by GhostlyOcam » Sun Sep 19, 2021 10:58 pm Prior to pandemic, Oscars only allow films that at least have a week of theatrical release in the US to be eligible (and Eva has none so far) and this rule was made in response to the ever-growing streaming-only films on Netflix and AmaPrime, although it was dropped since last year so streaming-only films are eligible for Oscars consideration, I feel like most people here already explains the logistics on why the chances for Eva are slim, so that's that. But with how well-received it is in its home country, there's a chance it could win big at the Japan Academy FIlm Prize next year (essentially Anno repeating history after Shin Godzilla) so better watch that space instead.
